prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response:
1. Hear ye, for in the third quadrant of our Lord's fiscal year, the Cloud didst yield forth a bounty of Recurring Revenues most Annual, swelling by some twenty and three parts in the hundred, a miracle surpassing even the multiplication of the loaves, though considerably less filling.

2. Our Churn, that foul pestilence which doth carry off subscribers in the night, was abated by the diligent labors of the Customer Success knights, who ride forth each morn armed with dashboards of flame and surveys of Net Promoter.

3. The EBITDA, a serpent of uncertain temperament described to me by the Chief of Finance as "adjusted," hath coiled itself into positivity for the first time since the Feast of Series B, whereupon all the engineers did feast upon catered sushi and weep into their standing desks.

4. Let it be inscribed upon vellum and also the Notion, that our pipeline runneth over with Qualified Leads numbering four thousand and twelve, though I confess I know not what a Lead is, nor why it must be Qualified, nor who among us shall be held accountable when the Sales Wizard inevitably sandbags the forecast.
